item.title: Hong Kong Exchange Tightens Rules to Avoid Auditor Shopping
item.description: Hong Kong listed companies must now obtain shareholder approval to change auditors, part of a regulatory push to bolster corporate governance and transparency in the $7.5 trillion market.

item.title: Australian Pension Chiefs Target Private Assets in European Trip
item.description: Australia’s biggest pension funds are heading to France and the UK this week in a fresh push for private market deals, just over a month after a similar US trip.

item.title: Malaysian Ringgit Set to Test New High for 2026, Strategists Say
item.description: The Malaysian ringgit is likely to retest its year-to-date peak versus the dollar thanks to strong fundamentals, according to strategists.

item.title: Asia Hedge Funds Log Big Losses From Iran War Before Truce Rally
item.description: To see how Asian hedge funds got blindsided by the Iran war, look no further than Trivest Advisors Ltd.

item.title: Australia’s Viva Sees Refinery Output at 90% Within Weeks
item.description: Viva Energy Group Ltd. shares fell after the company said its Geelong refinery in Australia will boost diesel, jet fuel and gasoline production to about 90% of capacity over the next few weeks after a major fire forced it to cut output.

item.title: Bill Ackman, The Weeknd and the Hunt for Universal’s Missing Billions
item.description: Bill Ackman has offered to buy Universal Music Group. What he really wants is for the stock to go up.

item.title: Singapore’s Model for Success Faces Test in a Less Global Era
item.description: Singapore’s growth is poised to moderate as its export-driven model is strained by geopolitical tensions and a fragmenting global trading system, though it could draw support from opportunities in the Middle East, according to Bloomberg Intelligence.

item.title: Asia Private Credit Debates Changes to Soothe Jittery Investors
item.description: Some of Asia’s private credit firms are mulling changes to the industry including longer lock-up periods and higher redemption caps after turmoil in the US in recent months increased scrutiny from investors and regulators across the region.

item.title: Philippine Central Banker Urges Banks to Promote FX Hedging
item.description: Philippine banks may have to push harder to convince companies to hedge their foreign exchange exposure as the war in Iran underscores the country’s vulnerability to geopolitical shocks, according to a central banker.

item.title: BofA, Jupiter See Upside for Asia Defense Stocks on Arms Buildup
item.description: Asian defense stocks are attracting renewed investor interest following a global arms buildup sparked by the Iran war, with some investors seeing the sector as a longer-term growth story rather than a short-term geopolitical trade.

item.title: New Zealand PM Luxon Holds Resolve as Polling Support Tumbles
item.description: New Zealand Prime Minister Christopher Luxon says he is not contemplating stepping down ahead of November’s election as opinion polls show support for him and his National Party slumping.

item.title: NEXTDC to Raise $1.1 Billion to Meet Data Center Demand
item.description: Australian data center operator NEXTDC Ltd. will undertake a A$1.5 billion ($1.1 billion) capital raising, boosting its coffers as demand for capacity at its facilities surges.

item.title: European Gas Futures Soar as Iran Closes Strait of Hormuz Again
item.description: European natural gas futures rose as Iran again closed the Strait of Hormuz, adding fears that the flow of energy from the Persian Gulf will remain choked.

item.title: Gold Falls as Renewed Hormuz Disruption Stokes Inflation Concern
item.description: Gold fell after vessels came under fire in the Strait of Hormuz at the weekend, renewing fears of energy-supply disruptions that have stoked inflation concerns during more than seven weeks of war in the Middle East.

item.title: US Stock Futures Fall, Oil Jumps on Iran Tensions: Markets Wrap
item.description: US equity-index futures slipped, oil jumped and the dollar rose as risk sentiment soured following a renewed flare-up in US-Iran tensions over the weekend and a continued standoff in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Oil Jumps After US Seizure of Iranian Ship Escalates Conflict
item.description: Oil soared after the US Navy seized an Iranian ship during a chaotic weekend that saw Tehran firing at vessels and reimposing controls in the Strait of Hormuz just hours after opening the waterway.

item.title: Hedge Fund Pay Spirals Even Higher in New Trader-Poaching Strategy
item.description: As the competition for talent reaches new extremes, ‘interception trades’ between rivals are heating up.

item.title: Point72, BlackRock-Backed Contest Seeks Japanese Banking Talent
item.description: Scores of Japanese college students have been obsessing over sales of toothpaste, diapers and chocolate. They have visited supermarkets and drugstores to photograph shelves, scrutinized price tags and noted which brands sit at eye level for maximum exposure.

item.title: UAE in Talks With US for Possible Financial Lifeline, WSJ Says
item.description: The United Arab Emirates has begun talks with the US about a financial backstop in case the Iran war plunges the country into further crisis, the Wall Street Journal reported, citing US officials it didn’t identify.

item.title: Traders Brace for Renewed Turmoil on Hormuz Standoff
item.description: Traders are preparing for a bumpy open to the week as a continued standoff around the Strait of Hormuz revives the uncertainty that Wall Street had been eager to look past, after a week that drove the S&P 500 Index to a record and pushed oil back toward $90 a barrel.

item.title: Trump Says US Military Seized An Iranian Vessel in Gulf of Oman
item.description: US Navy ship stopped Iranian-flagged cargo ship in its tracks “by blowing a hole in the engineroom” and US marines have custody of the ship, President Donald Trump says on Truth Social.

item.title: Next Catalyst for Treasury Bonds Is Named Kevin Warsh
item.description: Bond traders who’ve grown more optimistic about an end to the Iran conflict see their next catalyst coming from Capitol Hill, where Kevin Warsh will face questions as President Donald Trump’s pick to lead the Federal Reserve.

item.title: After Long Avoiding Politics, Commodity Traders Cozy Up to Trump
item.description: For decades, the world’s biggest commodity traders have sought to remain outside of politics, doing deals with anyone who has natural resources to buy or sell. Now, Donald Trump is changing the rules of the game.

item.title: Vitol Made About $2 Billion in First Quarter Despite War Losses
item.description: Vitol Group has told banks it made a first-quarter profit of roughly $2 billion, as the world’s top energy trader moves to reassure lenders over losses in some parts of its business due to the Iran war.

item.title: Apple’s Revamped Siri Interface in iOS 27 Is Hidden in WWDC Teaser
item.description: Also: Memory shortages could push back new Macs.

item.title: Option Traders Chasing Torrid Stock Rally Turn Focus to Earnings
item.description: Option traders have flipped from concern that the Iran war will sink the economy to fear of missing out on the torrid equity rebound. That shift away from macro risk to individual stocks is set to continue as corporate earnings take center stage.

item.title: Big Tech’s $4 Trillion Boomerang Powers S&P 500 to New Heights
item.description: Big Tech stocks are back and powering the S&P 500 Index to new highs, giving investors confidence that the latest equity market rally has room to run even as the risks from the war with Iran remain.

item.title: Hormuz Shipping Traffic Grinds to a Halt as Tensions Deepen
item.description: Observed transits of commercial ships through the Strait of Hormuz have come to a halt following a brief surge on Saturday as tensions ratcheted higher after vessels came under gunfire in the waterway and Iran warned against crossings.

item.title: Emerging-Market Bond Sales Are Soaring Again as Investors Dive Back Into Risk
item.description: Emerging-market bond sales are roaring back from last month’s doldrums, as issuers from Brazil to Turkey take advantage of rebounding markets to raise fresh financing.

item.title: China’s Orient Securities Deal to Create $86 Billion Brokerage
item.description: Two Shanghai government‑backed brokerages plan to merge in a deal that will create a firm with around $86 billion in assets, underscoring China’s push to consolidate the securities industry as it seeks to build world‑class investment banks.
